danger_signals.visual_form: Anthropomorphic robot criminals with distinct character designs
danger_signals.meaning: Active threat to town safety; enemy encounter imminent

danger_signals.visual_form: Train robberies, bank heists, saloon shootouts, cattle stampedes
danger_signals.meaning: Town under direct threat; law enforcement intervention required

danger_signals.visual_form: Bad Oil Gang members dressed as women/children or wearing masks
danger_signals.meaning: Deception threat; enemies hiding in plain sight; moral ambiguity in targeting

danger_signals.visual_form: Prison break sequences with visible tunnel exits and escaping figures
danger_signals.meaning: Loss of containment; gang regrouping for major assault

danger_signals.visual_form: Black Bart replacing Tin Star as sheriff; false narratives spread through town
danger_signals.meaning: Town government compromised; institutional failure of law enforcement

safety_signals.visual_form: Six-gun armed robot character with badge/sheriff authority visual markers
safety_signals.meaning: Law and order representative; player as protector of town

safety_signals.visual_form: Cash accumulation from successful enemy eliminations and objective completion
safety_signals.meaning: Town stabilization; progress toward securing East Driftwood

safety_signals.visual_form: Mo Crash (sidekick), Aluminum (horse), Mayor's daughter Maria
safety_signals.meaning: Social support network; civilian dependents requiring protection

safety_signals.visual_form: Saloons, banks, jails, newly built railroad in functional state
safety_signals.meaning: Economic and social stability maintained when structures aren't damaged

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Sheriff's office, jail facilities, town center locations

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Oil-themed criminal identity (gang leader Black Bart, Oil brothers Snake and Crude Johnson)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Disguises as women and children; individual gang members with distinct visual characteristics (Tiny Johnson, Bugsy Johnson, Lucky Johnson, The Kid Johnson, Snake Oil, Crude Oil, Black Bart)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Criminal hideouts, gang headquarters, train routes, desert locations

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Can appear as corrupted (misinformation spreaders hired by Black Bart)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Saloons, banks, town streets, domestic spaces

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Coach ride sequence with criminals attempting ambush; town appears normal but under immediate stress
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Tin Star receives rapid-fire mission assignments upon arrival; urgency escalates immediately

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Multiple criminal incidents visible: saloon shootouts, cattle stampedes, train robberies occurring across different locations
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Daily structure (Monday-Sunday) with shooting practice, action stages, and fast-draw confrontations; cash economy reflects town damage

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Bad Oil Gang members appearing as women and men in disguise; visual contradiction between appearance and threat level
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Town enforces 'Good Guy Code of the West' preventing shooting of women/children; enemies exploit this moral framework

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Black Bart visually assumes sheriff role; misinformation campaign spreads through town; false narrative about Tin Star murdering a child
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Tin Star stripped of badge and authority; knocked unconscious and abandoned in desert; civilian Mo kidnapped as leverage

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Tin Star operates disguised; Mo rescued; The Kid shown alive to townspeople; Black Bart's headquarters destroyed
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Final confrontation sequence with Black Bart reveals ultimate plot twist (Maria identity); ending varies by accumulated cash

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Three distinct ending states based on cash balance: below $750k (Maria rejects Tin Star), $750k-$999,999 (Maria marries Mo), over $1M (Maria revealed as Black Bart in disguise)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Ending cinematics change based on economic metrics; cash directly determines romantic/political resolution

environmental_storytelling.example: Railroad serves as both infrastructure (economic progress) and vulnerability (target for gang robberies); saloons as social spaces that become battlegrounds
Bad Oil Gang members named after oil-related concepts (Snake Oil, Crude Oil) and Johnson brothers; Tin Star as six-gun sheriff robot; Mo Crash as sidekick
'Good Guy Code of the West' physically manifests as restriction on player actions; environment (societal rules) actively constrains gameplay and narrative
Player loses cash by damaging town and wasting bullets; cash accumulation directly reflects town security and economic health; cash determines ending
Jail break with visible tunnel excavations shows infrastructure failure; inmates escape using disguises, demonstrating how deception compromises containment
Gang dresses as women to exploit moral codes; Maria revealed as Black Bart; masks and false appearances signal institutional deception

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Engage in robberies, shootouts, stampede triggers; use disguises to evade moral constraints; coordinate group actions against town
Orchestrates misinformation campaign; hires civilians to spread false narratives; assumes sheriff position; maintains deception until final confrontation
Cannot be directly targeted due to 'Good Guy Code' despite being experienced gunfighter; used as emotional leverage against Tin Star
Quick-draw confrontations; imprisonment and escape; participation in gang operations
Slow but deadly in confrontations; represents different threat profile through inverse skill distribution
Enforces law, saves town, faces fast-draws daily; operates disguised after authority loss; culminates in final dual confrontation with Black Bart
Accept false narratives from Black Bart's hired spreader; require rescue (Mo kidnapped); respond to Tin Star's public demonstrations of truth (The Kid alive)
Initially functions as sidekick; kidnapped as gang leverage; later revealed to inherit wealth from brother's will; becomes alternative romantic option based on ending conditions

notes: Tin Star communicates world-state primarily through: (1) daily mission structure reflecting escalating threat levels, (2) cash economy as dual indicator of town health and player performance, (3) visual/moral constraint system ('Good Guy Code') that creates narrative tension, (4) character design and naming convention (oil/robot/western mashup) that establishes faction identity, (5) disguise and deception mechanics that manifest institutional vulnerability, (6) ending variation system that ties political/romantic outcomes directly to economic performance. The game's manifestation layer treats economics as a world-state variable: cash spent on town damage reduces security rating and changes ending narrative. Authority (sheriff badge) functions as visible status indicator vulnerable to corruption. Deception appears both as gameplay mechanic (enemies in disguise) and narrative theme (institutional lies, false identity revelations). The 7-day structure with daily rituals (practice, action stages, fast-draws) creates rhythm that manifests increasing desperation as authority collapses midway through narrative.
